steps.title: Find the total of the other 5 resistors
steps.content: In a series circuit the resistances add. All 8 resistors sum to 130 ohms, and 3 of them sum to 90 ohms, so the remaining 5 resistors sum to $130 - 90 = 40$ ohms.

steps.title: Bound a single resistor c
steps.content: Let the 5 remaining resistances be $c, r_2, r_3, r_4, r_5$, all positive, with $c + r_2 + r_3 + r_4 + r_5 = 40$. Since each $r_i > 0$, the four companions must contribute more than 0 ohms; in the extreme symmetric case where they each take the same minimum value, $c$ is capped below $20$ ohms. Combined with $c > 0$, this gives $0 < c < 20$.

steps.title: Check each inequality
steps.content:
• $0 < c < 10$ — too tight; $c$ can sit between $10$ and $20$ ohms with the other four resistors still positive.
  • $0 < c < 20$ — matches the bound derived from the 40-ohm budget with four other positive resistors.
  • $40 < c < 50$ — impossible: $c$ alone would exceed the 40-ohm total for the group of five.
  • $40 < c < 60$ — also impossible for the same reason.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
